_comment: Cross-implementation golden vectors for field-level AES-256-GCM encryption.
_contract: All three platform implementations MUST produce byte-identical output for the same inputs:
_impls: JVM (Java 21 JCE AES/GCM/NoPadding) — this file was generated from this impl
Node (Node.js crypto module, createCipheriv) — LAUNCH-GATE: must verify before prod
Device (react-native-quick-crypto / BoringSSL) — LAUNCH-GATE: EAS build required
_regenerate: Only regenerate by bumping the envelope version (0x01 → 0x02 + 'v2:' AAD prefix). Changing these bytes without updating mobile/Node fixtures silently breaks cross-platform decryption.

vectors.description: Canonical golden vector: plaintext 'hello', all-zero 32-byte DEK, all-zero 12-byte IV, standard AAD
notes: base64 variant: RFC 4648 standard (padded, '=' suffix) — NOT base64url
tag binds: accountId + collection + recordId + fieldName — any mismatch → tag fail
ciphertext length = plaintext length (GCM is a stream cipher, no padding)
generated by: JVM Java 21, JCE AES/GCM/NoPadding, 128-bit tag, 2026-07-06

nist_kat_reference._comment: NIST SP 800-38D known-answer test vectors proving JVM AES-GCM is spec-correct
